title: 03 the camera joins tina scene
prompt: REFERENCE AUTHORITY — Images 1 and 2 lock Tina, wardrobe, intact car, rear seat, product moment, and live-action identity. The surrounding world may be replaced. ONE RULE — The cinema camera filming the commercial grows elegant folded-paper arms and legs, climbs through Tina's open passenger window, and starts physically rebuilding the photographed neighborhood into a portable theatrical world for her. A second unseen viewpoint films this event. FRAME — Playful wide from outside the intact car. Tina holds the exact bite pose, delighted rather than frightened. A full-size professional cinema camera is halfway through the window on articulated white paper limbs. With one hand it lifts the real live-action street upward like a broad scenic flat; immediately behind that moving edge, houses, sky, trees, and traffic become layered paper- theatre scenery with visible folds, tabs, and practical shadows. Its other hand unfolds one paper cloud above Tina. Keep the camera unmistakably a camera, without face or mascot eyes. VISUAL PROOF — camera body, paper limbs, its hands physically holding the live-action-to-paper boundary, Tina's ordinary car crossing both materials. AVOID — animated camera logo, robot camera, camera-headed human, car transformation, giant hand from outside, paper flower, movie-set crew.

resolved_params.system_prompt: Create one extraordinary but coherent cinematic Peelerz commercial styleframe, never a collage, contact sheet, storyboard grid, or design sheet. Ben's brief is an open canvas: favor a bold whole- world creative situation over improving any existing car-peel, parachute, or mango-mecha idea. Preserve only the explicitly declared talent/location authorities. Make the prompt's single impossible rule visible at world scale with one dominant verb, clear spatial organization, and a youthful sense of wonder. The result can be excessive, mythic, psychedelic, deadpan, or impossible, but must remain benign, appetizing, and not preschool-coded. No car opening into petals, no parachute, no robot/mecha armor, no construction-helper crew, no rainbow arch, no generic cheering gummy crowd, no captions, labels, borders, watermarks, gore, weapons, or invented package copy.
